terms.definition: Transparent avascular front layer of the eye providing about two-thirds of the eye's refractive power.
terms.exampleSentence: Slit-lamp examination revealed a small superficial abrasion of the central cornea.
terms.contextNotes: Refractive surface; assessed with slit lamp and keratometer.

terms.definition: Tough white outer fibrous coat of the eyeball that maintains the globe's shape.
terms.exampleSentence: The sclera was injected and tender on palpation, raising suspicion of scleritis.
terms.contextNotes: Continuous with the cornea at the limbus.

terms.definition: Pigmented muscular diaphragm controlling pupil size and the amount of light entering the eye.
terms.exampleSentence: The iris was sluggish to respond to light, suggesting a relative afferent pupillary defect.
terms.contextNotes: Examined for shape, colour and reactivity.

terms.definition: Central aperture in the iris through which light passes to reach the retina.
terms.exampleSentence: The right pupil was fixed and dilated at 6 mm with no direct or consensual response.
terms.contextNotes: Test direct, consensual and accommodation responses.

terms.definition: Transparent biconvex intraocular structure that fine-tunes focus by changing curvature during accommodation.
terms.exampleSentence: Opacification of the crystalline lens was consistent with a nuclear sclerotic cataract.
terms.contextNotes: Loses elasticity with age, causing presbyopia.

terms.definition: Light-sensitive neural layer at the back of the eye that converts light into electrical signals.
terms.exampleSentence: Dilated fundoscopy showed scattered microaneurysms across the temporal retina.
terms.contextNotes: Imaged with OCT and fundus photography.

terms.definition: Small central retinal area responsible for high-acuity central vision and colour discrimination.
terms.exampleSentence: OCT revealed drusen at the macula, supporting a diagnosis of early dry AMD.
terms.contextNotes: Use Amsler grid for symptom monitoring.

terms.definition: Tiny depression at the centre of the macula containing densely packed cones for sharpest vision.
terms.exampleSentence: Foveal reflex was absent, raising concern for early macular pathology.
terms.contextNotes: Region of highest visual acuity.

terms.definition: Region where retinal ganglion axons exit the eye to form the optic nerve; physiologically blind spot.
terms.exampleSentence: The optic disc appeared pale with a cup-to-disc ratio of 0.7, suspicious for glaucoma.
terms.contextNotes: Cup-to-disc ratio is a key glaucoma marker.

terms.definition: Clear gelatinous substance filling the posterior segment between the lens and the retina.
terms.exampleSentence: Pigmented cells in the vitreous humour suggested a retinal tear with vitreous haemorrhage.
terms.contextNotes: Liquefies with age, causing PVD and floaters.

terms.definition: Clear fluid in the anterior and posterior chambers, produced by the ciliary body, that nourishes the cornea and lens.
terms.exampleSentence: Reduced outflow of aqueous humour through the trabecular meshwork raised intraocular pressure.
terms.contextNotes: Imbalance underlies most glaucomas.

terms.definition: Ring of tissue behind the iris containing the ciliary muscle and producing aqueous humour.
terms.exampleSentence: Spasm of the ciliary body during refraction was managed with cycloplegic drops.
terms.contextNotes: Drives accommodation via zonular tension.

terms.definition: Vascular pigmented layer between the retina and sclera supplying blood to the outer retina.
terms.exampleSentence: OCT showed thickening of the choroid suggestive of central serous chorioretinopathy.
terms.contextNotes: Site of CNV in wet AMD.

terms.definition: System producing and draining tears, comprising the lacrimal gland, puncta, canaliculi, sac and nasolacrimal duct.
terms.exampleSentence: Obstruction of the lacrimal apparatus was confirmed on syringing and probing.
terms.contextNotes: Epiphora is the cardinal symptom of obstruction.

terms.definition: Thin transparent mucous membrane covering the sclera and inner eyelids.
terms.exampleSentence: The bulbar conjunctiva was injected with a mucopurulent discharge present at the lid margins.
terms.contextNotes: Inspect with eversion of the upper lid.

terms.definition: Anatomical term for the eyelid; protects the globe and spreads the tear film with each blink.
terms.exampleSentence: Mild oedema of the upper palpebra was noted following the chalazion incision.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective form 'palpebral' is more common in records.

terms.definition: Dilated upper portion of the nasolacrimal duct lying in the lacrimal fossa, collecting tears from the canaliculi.
terms.exampleSentence: Pressure over the lacrimal sac produced reflux of mucopurulent material, indicating dacryocystitis.
terms.contextNotes: Massage technique taught for infant duct obstruction.

terms.definition: Circular venous-like channel at the limbus that drains aqueous humour from the trabecular meshwork into the episcleral veins.
terms.exampleSentence: Selective laser trabeculoplasty aims to improve outflow into the canal of Schlemm.
terms.contextNotes: Central to conventional aqueous outflow pathway.

terms.definition: Sieve-like tissue at the iridocorneal angle through which aqueous humour drains into the canal of Schlemm.
terms.exampleSentence: Pigment deposition in the trabecular meshwork was visible on gonioscopy.
terms.contextNotes: Resistance here drives most primary open-angle glaucoma.

terms.definition: Photoreceptor cells concentrated at the macula responsible for colour vision and high-acuity daylight vision.
terms.exampleSentence: Loss of cones at the fovea was suspected on adaptive optics imaging.
terms.contextNotes: Three subtypes: S, M and L cones.

terms.definition: Photoreceptor cells in the peripheral retina responsible for low-light and peripheral vision.
terms.exampleSentence: Difficulty driving at night was attributed to early rod dysfunction in retinitis pigmentosa.
terms.contextNotes: Mediate scotopic (night) vision via rhodopsin.

terms.definition: Cranial nerve II carrying visual information from the retina to the brain.
terms.exampleSentence: Optic nerve atrophy was evident, with marked thinning on the OCT RNFL scan.
terms.contextNotes: Assess with VA, fields, colour vision and RAPD.

terms.definition: Transitional ring where the cornea meets the sclera and conjunctiva, containing corneal stem cells.
terms.exampleSentence: Injection was most marked at the limbus, suggesting anterior uveitis rather than conjunctivitis.
terms.contextNotes: Ciliary flush points to intraocular inflammation.

terms.definition: Fluid-filled space between the cornea and the iris containing aqueous humour.
terms.exampleSentence: Cells and flare were graded 2+ in the anterior chamber, consistent with acute uveitis.
terms.contextNotes: Shallow AC is a risk factor for angle-closure.

terms.definition: Suspensory fibres connecting the ciliary body to the lens capsule, transmitting tension during accommodation.
terms.exampleSentence: Weakness of the zonules of Zinn caused subluxation of the lens in this Marfan patient.
terms.contextNotes: Loss of integrity complicates cataract surgery.

terms.definition: Single layer of pigmented cells beneath the neural retina supporting photoreceptors and recycling visual pigments.
terms.exampleSentence: Mottling of the retinal pigment epithelium on autofluorescence indicated dry AMD.
terms.contextNotes: RPE dysfunction underlies many retinal dystrophies.

terms.definition: Serrated junction between the retina and the ciliary body marking the anterior limit of the neural retina.
terms.exampleSentence: A retinal dialysis was identified at the ora serrata in the inferotemporal quadrant.
terms.contextNotes: Common site of traumatic retinal dialysis.

terms.definition: Six muscles per eye controlling globe movement: medial, lateral, superior and inferior recti, superior and inferior obliques.
terms.exampleSentence: Examination of the extraocular muscles revealed limitation on abduction of the right eye.
terms.contextNotes: Test in nine cardinal positions of gaze.

terms.definition: Sebaceous glands embedded in the tarsal plates secreting the lipid layer of the tear film.
terms.exampleSentence: Capping of the Meibomian glands and frothy tears were consistent with evaporative dry eye.
terms.contextNotes: MGD is the leading cause of evaporative dry eye.

terms.definition: Innermost layer of the choroid lying between the choriocapillaris and the retinal pigment epithelium.
terms.exampleSentence: Breaks in Bruch's membrane allowed choroidal neovascularisation in wet AMD.
terms.contextNotes: Pathological breaks underlie wet AMD CNV.
